stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) → x ≐ ⊠x
priority_rationale: This is a local support lemma with little independent summary impact.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) → x ≐ □x
priority_rationale: This is another local support lemma whose value is mainly internal to nearby proofs.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊠x) ↔ x := by False
summary_delta: This candidate is rejected because it is malformed and would not meet the quality bar.
priority_rationale: The statement is malformed and cannot contribute to the frontier.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] {h : HenkinFixpoint α}, h.1 ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
priority_rationale: This is a witness-level Henkin fact rather than a theory-changing bridge.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α], Consistent α → (Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ¬⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α))
priority_rationale: This older local criterion has been superseded by sharper verified Gödel existence theorems.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent g.1 (⊠(⊤ : α))
summary_delta: The solved result turns Gödel fixed points from arbitrary witnesses into a canonical equivalence class. Packaging existence together with that canonical representative would upgrade the current theory from mere existence to canonical form, which is a reusable interface for later bridges and avoids falling back to witness-specific arguments.
priority_rationale: Canonical representative packaging is weaker than the current push toward exact subtype equivalences.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α], Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This comparison is already overtaken by stronger positive and negative results relating Gödel and Henkin points.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ¬ ((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊥))
summary_delta: The current verified theory has a modal existence criterion and a one-way consistency-sensitive irreputability consequence, but no bridge between them. A full equivalence would connect existence to provability-side boundary data exactly where the theory state says the frontier is missing, and it would clarify whether the canonical object `⊠⊤` captures the obstruction to existence under consistency.
priority_rationale: This provability-side bridge has been undercut by later exact Gödel criteria and counterexample evidence.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ ((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊥)
summary_delta: This sharpens the current provability-side bridge by testing whether consistency is actually needed to rule out refutability of `⊠⊤`. Resolving it would directly advance the stated bottleneck of turning the modal existence theorem into a condition expressed purely at the level of `⊠⊤`.
priority_rationale: This is a narrow one-way sharpening with limited reuse compared to current interface targets.

stmt: Any two-element linearly ordered instance of `ACR + Prov + Reft + APS + C5` with `□ = id` and `⊠⊥ = ⊤`, `⊠⊤ = ⊥` satisfies `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`
summary_delta: The verified proof used one concrete two-point model; abstracting that witness into a reusable schema would turn a single example into a counterexample-construction principle. That is valuable beyond the present theorem because it gives a controlled mechanism for future separation and threshold arguments rather than another isolated existential witness.
priority_rationale: The queue no longer needs more specialized two-point counterexample schemata.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ((⊤ : α) ≐ □(⊤ : α))
summary_delta: The verified proof exposed that the solved implication is driven by a canonical Henkin witness at `⊤`, not by any Gödel witness. Making that explicit upgrades a comparison theorem into a central structural fact about `□` at `⊤`, and it clarifies that the Henkin side collapses under `C5` for theory-level reasons rather than by a local witness argument.
priority_rationale: This is a local structural fact whose theoretical payoff is now modest.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*), ACR α ∧ ACR.Prov α ∧ ACR.Reft α ∧ ACR.APS α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: Now that the implication is proved under `C5` by a much stronger argument, the sharp next boundary question is whether `C5` is genuinely necessary for the Gödel-to-Henkin bridge. A separator of this form would isolate `C5` as an essential threshold assumption rather than a proof convenience, directly advancing the current bottleneck about exact `C5` necessity.
priority_rationale: Separator hunting around `C5` is now lower value than exact interface theorems.

stmt: For every `α` with `ACR α` and `ACR.Reft α`, `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` is equivalent to `∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g`, and hence `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤` is exactly the condition that `⊠⊤` itself is a Gödel fixed point.
summary_delta: The counterexample exposed that the modal equation does not merely coexist with Gödel existence; it canonically identifies `⊠⊤` as the witness. Making that explicit upgrades the failed existential into a reusable structural criterion stated directly on `⊠⊤`, matching the current frontier's need for a clean existence test rather than another local contradiction.
priority_rationale: A stronger form of this hoped-for canonical criterion has already been refuted.

stmt: There exists `α` with `ACR α`, `ACR.Prov α`, `ACR.Reft α`, and `ACR.APS α` such that `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` holds but `¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)`.
summary_delta: This is the sharp threshold question now suggested by the refutation: the forward implication from the modal equation to existence is already forced without `C5`, so the only remaining role for `C5` is the converse direction. A verified example here would isolate that necessity cleanly and would be stronger, more informative boundary evidence than re-asking for the already-refuted impossible combination.
priority_rationale: This is mainly another separator question in an already overexplored direction.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊥)
summary_delta: The verified one-point model suggests a direct counterexample to the hoped-for bridge from Gödel existence to non-refutability of `⊠⊤`. Resolving this would sharply separate the modal existence criterion from the provability-side obstruction and would clarify that consistency, not mere existence, is doing essential work in the current irrefutability theorem.
priority_rationale: This local counterexample target no longer changes the main summary.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*), ACR α ∧ ACR.Prov α ∧ ACR.Reft α ∧ ACR.APS α ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The proof suggests that `C5` may be the mechanism forcing Henkin existence itself. Producing an `APS`-level model with no Henkin fixed point would turn the solved result into a threshold statement about the ambient axioms, giving reusable boundary evidence about when `□` can or cannot admit fixed points at all.
priority_rationale: Henkin-nonexistence examples without stronger structural content are no longer central.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [Subsingleton α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α], Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The solved result shows that one-point collapse forces Gödel existence for purely structural reasons, not because of the APS/C5 frontier. Asking whether the same collapse also forces Henkin existence would turn the current singleton witness into a reusable degenerate-model schema for both fixed-point notions, which directly helps separate genuinely below-`C5` consistent phenomena from artifacts of total collapse.
priority_rationale: Subsingleton behavior is already structurally understood through inconsistency classifications.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [Subsingleton α] [ACR α] [ACR.Reft α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
summary_delta: The proof picked `⊤` as a Gödel witness because subsingleton collapse makes every potential witness canonically the same. Making that uniqueness-up-to-equivalence explicit would upgrade the solved theorem from mere existence to a structural classification of degenerate Gödel models, which is exactly the kind of reusable counterexample principle the current theory state says is missing.
priority_rationale: This is a degenerate-model uniqueness fact with limited additional reach.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The current frontier explicitly asks whether the Gödel-to-Henkin comparison really needs `C5`. A consistent model of Gödel existence without any Henkin fixed point would be a sharp structural separation, stronger and more reusable than another local failure of canonicity at `⊠⊤`.
priority_rationale: This consistent separator target is still primarily another existence-style counterexample request.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
summary_delta: This is the clean next separator after the refutation: it asks whether the failure of the converse from Gödel existence to the top-box equation can already occur in consistent APS models, rather than only through degenerate or inconsistent behavior. Resolving it would directly classify whether the remaining obstruction is genuinely about `C5` or partly about consistency.
priority_rationale: This is a separator decoration that does not promise summary-level change.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((fun z => □z)^[n]) g.1 = y}
summary_delta: The solved result shows that any Gödel witness with a finite forward `□`-orbit would already force a Henkin point, so any consistent Gödel-without-Henkin separator must be genuinely infinitary. Establishing whether such a model exists would directly sharpen the main below-`C5` boundary and separate finite-orbit obstructions from truly infinite counterexample behavior.
priority_rationale: Adding infinitary orbit decoration to a separator model is exactly the kind of overexplored refinement to down-rank.

stmt: Assume `⊠` is constant in an `APS`. Determine whether `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)` is equivalent to `c ≐ □c`, where `c` is the constant value of `⊠`.
summary_delta: The failed statement tried to force Henkin failure from a fixed-point-free `□`, and the counterexample shows that constant reflection ties the whole question to the single comparison between `c` and `□c`. A clean criterion here would isolate the Henkin boundary for the constant-reflection regime and would be a sharper structural lesson than another isolated counterexample.
priority_rationale: Constant-reflection theory is already organized by monotonicity-plus-bounds, so this specialized criterion is not pressing.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∀ m n : ℕ, m < n → ((fun z => □z)^[m]) g.1 ≠ ((fun z => □z)^[n]) g.1
summary_delta: The proof log identifies finite-orbit collapse as the actual obstruction. Turning that into a no-repetition theorem gives a sharper boundary than mere infinitude: separator models would have to exhibit genuinely aperiodic `□`-dynamics on every Gödel witness, not just large orbits. That isolates a reusable infinitary obstruction rather than another local corollary.
priority_rationale: This is a sharpened separator-side orbit constraint, but it remains a local derivative of existing orbit results.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∀ m n : ℕ, m < n → ¬ (((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) g.1)
summary_delta: Now that repetition on a Gödel orbit is known to force Henkin existence, the separator frontier shifts from mere infinitude to genuine aperiodicity modulo `≐`. This candidate sharpens the obstruction in exactly the way the current theory state asks for: separator models would have to avoid not only equality repeats but any collapse inside the preorder-equivalence classes.
priority_rationale: This is another separator-side anti-periodicity variant with limited new structural reach.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.C5 α], Monotone (fun x : α => □x) → ((∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR, ∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α _ hR x = @Top.top α _) ↔ ((⊤ : α) ≤ □⊤))
summary_delta: This turns the solved fixed-constant criterion into a sharp C5 boundary theorem centered on the canonical element `⊤`, eliminating the auxiliary existential over `c`. It is not just a cosmetic corollary: it would show that, in the main rigid regime already present in the theory, constant reflection is controlled exactly by the top post-fixpoint condition, giving a reusable bridge from extension existence to a single canonical `□`-condition.
priority_rationale: This sits inside an already organized constant-reflection extension theory and is not a frontier bottleneck.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (¬ Monotone (fun x : α => □x)) → ¬ ∃ c : α, ∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR, ∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α _ hR x = c
summary_delta: The counterexample mechanism is not really about one bad constant but about the impossibility of extending a nonmonotone `□` to any `APS` at all. Isolating that obstruction as a standalone theorem would give a reusable counterexample principle for the whole constant-reflection program and cleanly separate the global `□`-compatibility issue from the local post-fixed-point condition on `c`.
priority_rationale: Nonmonotonicity obstructing constant reflection is useful but already close to settled extension criteria.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y ↔ x = y) → (∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR) ↔ ∃ t : α, t = ⊤ ∧ t = □t
summary_delta: The verified counterexample shows that in the equality-order regime the real boundary is not the existence of an arbitrary `□`-fixed point, but whether `⊤` itself is `□`-fixed. This gives a sharper extension criterion than the failed target, turns the counterexample into a reusable classification of when an `APS` expansion exists at all, and directly advances the current bottleneck of replacing local witness tests by exact extension boundaries.
priority_rationale: This equality-order extension criterion is fairly specialized and downstream of known constant-reflection theory.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α), Monotone (fun x : α => □x) ∧ ¬ ∃ c : α, (⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c
summary_delta: The solved criterion has two genuinely separate ingredients. A model with monotone `□` but no postfixed element above `⊤` would show that the witness condition is a sharp additional boundary, not a hidden consequence of monotonicity, and would strengthen the counterexample side of the constant-reflection extension theory.
priority_rationale: This separator for ingredients of the constant-reflection criterion is too local.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α), (∃ c : α, (⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c) ∧ ¬ Monotone (fun x : α => □x)
summary_delta: This is the complementary separator for the new classification. It would show that having a local postfixed witness above `⊤` does not force the global order-theoretic compatibility needed for constant reflection, isolating monotonicity as an independent obstruction and giving a reusable counterexample template.
priority_rationale: This complementary separator is likewise a local decomposition of an already organized theory.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1 = y}
summary_delta: This lifts the solved one-step orbit obstruction to the positive-iterate regime that the agenda explicitly values. It would say that consistent separator models cannot hide finite stabilization at any composite `□`-scale, not only at step size one, creating a stronger preservation/interface statement for generalized composites and clarifying whether infinitary behavior is truly robust rather than an artifact of the first iterate.
priority_rationale: Positive-iterate orbit infinitude is a repetitive strengthening of known separator constraints.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ m n : ℕ, m < n → ((fun z => □z)^[m]) (⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[n]) (⊠(⊤ : α)) ∧ ¬ (((fun z => □z)^[n]) (⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[m]) (⊠(⊤ : α)))
summary_delta: With `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤`, the element `⊠⊤` is the canonical Gödel witness, so the counterexample points toward studying its forward `□`-chain directly. Showing that any consistent no-Henkin separator forces a strictly ascending orbit would sharpen the frontier from mere infinitude to an exact order-theoretic obstruction and keep `□`, `⊠⊤`, and `⊠⊠⊤` at the center.
priority_rationale: Strictly ascending orbit claims are too specific to one separator proof path.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x : α} {m n : ℕ}, x ≤ □x → m < n → ((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) x → ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
summary_delta: The verified proof actually uses only an inflationary starting point and monotonicity of `□`, not the full Gödel-fixpoint package except to supply `x ≤ □x`. Isolating that order-theoretic core would turn the just-solved result into a reusable bridge theorem for the whole `□`-dynamics frontier rather than a one-off fact about Gödel witnesses.
priority_rationale: This extracts the reusable order-theoretic core behind the solved orbit-repeat theorem and could simplify several nearby arguments.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (¬ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h) → ∀ {m n : ℕ} {g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α}, m < n → ¬ (((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) g.1)
summary_delta: This specializes the new orbit criterion to the main below-`C5` separator regime, where the open question is whether consistent Gödel existence can persist without Henkin existence. It is not a local restatement: it isolates the exact dynamical burden any such separator model must satisfy, turning the solved theorem into a structural constraint on the frontier case the theory most wants to understand.
priority_rationale: This is a specialized separator-side restatement of already known orbit-repeat consequences.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x : α}, x ≤ □x → (¬ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h) → ∀ {m n : ℕ}, m < n → ¬ (((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) x)
summary_delta: This is the sharp contrapositive boundary exposed by the verified proof: in the absence of Henkin fixed points, every inflationary orbit must be equivalence-aperiodic. It upgrades the current coarse infinitary obstruction into a canonical dynamical normal form, matching the theory state's request for a stronger criterion than mere infinitude.
priority_rationale: Equivalence-aperiodicity without Henkin points is sharp but still mainly a local dynamical corollary.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ boxk k h) ↔ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
summary_delta: The solved theorem now shows that finite positive `□^k`-orbit behavior produces a `□^k`-fixed point. The next sharp boundary question is whether such higher-iterate fixed points are genuinely new or collapse exactly to ordinary Henkin fixed points. Resolving this would convert the composite-iterate result into an exact classification of when positive `□`-composites add new fixed-point phenomena, directly advancing the fixed-point existence frontier rather than adding another local orbit lemma.
priority_rationale: Collapsing all positive-iterate fixed points to ordinary Henkin fixed points would unify a genuine fragment of the iterate theory.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ k : ℕ, 0 < k → ¬ ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h
priority_rationale: This is another separator-side no-iterate-fixed-point consequence rather than a new bridge.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x → Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y)}
priority_rationale: This is a technical iterate-orbit extension of already known finite-orbit implications.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∀ k : ℕ, 0 < k → Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1 = y)}
priority_rationale: This adds heavy infinitary decoration to a separator existence problem and is overexplored.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ h : α, h ≐ □h) ↔ ∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)
summary_delta: This turns the solved repeat-implies-`□^k`-fixpoint mechanism into an exact dynamical criterion for ordinary Henkin existence, which is the reusable bridge the current frontier is missing. It is stronger than another local iterate lemma because it classifies Henkin existence by visible positive-iterate orbit behavior and directly advances the agenda of organizing composite dynamics into a single characterization.
priority_rationale: An exact dynamical criterion for Henkin existence would be useful, but this formulation is broader and less direct than the current best per-`k` targets.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∀ k : ℕ, 0 < k → ¬ ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h
priority_rationale: This is another separator existence statement with iterate-fixed-point decoration.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This is a clean aggregate iterate-fixed-point characterization of Henkin existence, though it is less sharp than a per-`k` theorem.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: This per-`k` version is largely a support reformulation of the iterate-orbit program.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)
priority_rationale: This is another separator example request with little theory-wide leverage.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤))
priority_rationale: This exact equivalence is already verified as a derived theorem.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)
priority_rationale: This is an example request directly opposed to the verified exact Gödel criterion.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y)}) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This is a technical finite-orbit implication already close to established results.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: Positive-iterate fixed points implying Henkin existence is already part of the established iterate story.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ (g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α) (k m n : ℕ), 0 < k → m < n → ¬ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)
priority_rationale: This is a separator-side anti-repeat generalization with modest standalone value.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ¬ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x
summary_delta: This is a sharp separator-side boundary statement exposed by the solved bridge: if Henkin points fail in a consistent Gödel setting, then no positive iterate of □ can acquire a fixed point. It isolates the obstruction in a cleaner and more reusable way than individual nonrepetition lemmas, and it would clarify whether all positive-iterate collapse failures are genuinely uniform across k.
priority_rationale: Uniform no-iterate-fixed-point behavior in separators is structurally secondary to the positive iterate characterizations.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x)
summary_delta: This is the main positive-iterate collapse statement still missing from the frontier: it upgrades a one-way fixed-point bridge to an exact per-k characterization of Henkin existence and would unify all positive box-iterate fixed-point phenomena into the ordinary Henkin notion.
priority_rationale: This is the clean per-`k` characterization that would compress the fragmented iterate theory into one exact reusable interface.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)))
summary_delta: The solved result supplied the forward half of an exact composite-dynamics criterion. Completing this equivalence would directly realize the theory state's stated goal of upgrading one-step orbit-repeat characterizations to full per-k criteria, giving a reusable structural description of Henkin existence in terms of positive-iterate orbit dynamics on Gödel points.
priority_rationale: A per-`k` orbit-repeat criterion is important, but the Gödel-gated formulation is less definitive than the nongated iterate target.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1) ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)))
priority_rationale: This is a weaker Gödel-gated restatement of the same iterate-orbit direction.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α)
priority_rationale: This one-sided inequality is a minor consequence-style refinement with limited reuse.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1))) ↔ (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)))
summary_delta: The counterexample shows that the failed equivalence breaks exactly because Henkin existence does not force any Gödel witness. This repaired criterion isolates that obstruction cleanly and would turn the false statement into an exact boundary theorem for the Gödel-orbit formulation.
priority_rationale: This repaired equivalence still stays too close to a previously refuted local formulation.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∀ m n : ℕ, m < n → ¬ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1) ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1))
priority_rationale: This is another technical separator-side anti-repeat statement.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x) ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)))
priority_rationale: This is a batch-style restatement of the iterate-orbit criterion with no extra structural gain.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: This duplicates the central content of the stronger per-`k` iterate-fixed-point target.

stmt: ∃ α : Type*, ∃ _ : ACR α, ∃ _ : ACR.Prov α, ∃ _ : ACR.Reft α, ∃ _ : ACR.APS α, ∃ _ : ACR.C5 α, ACR.Consistent α ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x
summary_delta: This sharpens the solved separator from per-k witness existence to total collapse of the □-dynamics. It would show that even the strongest possible positive-iterate fixed-point regime does not recover Gödel existence, so any converse bridge must use genuinely stronger structure than iterate-fixedness alone.
priority_rationale: Total `□`-collapse without Gödel points is another separator decoration already subsumed by broader counterexample families.

stmt: ∃ α : Type*, ∃ _ : ACR α, ∃ _ : ACR.Prov α, ∃ _ : ACR.Reft α, ∃ _ : ACR.APS α, ∃ _ : ACR.C5 α, ACR.Consistent α ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ (∀ x : α, ⊠⊠x ≐ x) ∧ ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x
summary_delta: A natural repair attempt after the solved example is to impose an involutive reflection law on ⊠. Showing that Gödel nonexistence still persists under both trivial □-dynamics and involutive ⊠ would isolate the obstruction more cleanly and rule out a prominent structural route to restoring Gödel existence.
priority_rationale: Adding involutive `⊠` to the same separator pattern is even more decorative than structural.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (¬⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α))
summary_delta: After this counterexample, the main missing issue is how to recover Gödel existence from a reusable structural condition rather than assume it ad hoc. This converse-style bridge targets the below-C5 bottleneck directly and would connect the top-box interface to the existence side of the repaired dynamical criteria.
priority_rationale: This converse from irrefutability to Gödel existence has already been refuted.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1) = y}
priority_rationale: This is another iterate-orbit infinitude restatement in an overexplored separator direction.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)
priority_rationale: This follows immediately from the verified exact Gödel existence criterion.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: This duplicates the substantive content of the sharper per-`k` iterate-fixed-point characterization.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → IsTotal α (· ≤ ·) → (¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ Even (Fintype.card α))
summary_delta: The verified result gives the existence half on finite chains for every even size. Turning that into an exact parity criterion in the same chain regime would upgrade a witness family into a reusable boundary theorem, directly serving the agenda’s finite-versus-infinite and odd-versus-even pressure instead of adding another isolated example.
priority_rationale: Parity classification in finite trivial-`□` chains is explicitly overexplored and has already suffered decisive counterexample pressure.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)))
priority_rationale: This is a batch restatement of the iterate-orbit characterization with no new conceptual role.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → Odd (Fintype.card α) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The solved construction shows that even cardinality can support consistent Gödel-free models with trivial `□`. The natural converse boundary is whether odd finite size forces a Gödel point in this same `□`-collapsed setting; resolving that would identify parity as a genuine obstruction rather than a feature of one construction, which is exactly the kind of counterexample-theory sharpening the frontier needs.
priority_rationale: Odd-cardinality forcing in the finite trivial-`□` regime is heavily down-ranked by the current counterexample landscape.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → IsTotal α (· ≤ ·) → Odd (Fintype.card α) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This is another parity-focused finite-chain claim in an overworked direction.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → IsTotal α (· ≤ ·) → Odd (Fintype.card α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
priority_rationale: This is a specialized parity-side consequence with little frontier impact.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)))
priority_rationale: This ungated orbit-repeat characterization drops the inflationary hypothesis, but it remains less central than the iterate-fixed-point interface.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR α] [ACR.Reft α], Function.Involutive (fun x : α => ⊠x) → Odd (Fintype.card α) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: Odd/even finite-cardinality claims for involutive `⊠` are too specialized relative to the current summary needs.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR α] [ACR.Reft α], Function.Involutive (fun x : α => ⊠x) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → Even (Fintype.card α)
priority_rationale: This is the parity-side converse of another low-value finite involution claim.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1 = y}) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
priority_rationale: This finite-orbit implication is already close to known repeat-implies-Henkin results.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → IsTotal α (· ≤ ·) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
priority_rationale: This one-way canonicalization is weaker than the verified exact criterion under `C5`.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)))
priority_rationale: This exact equivalence is already verified.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) → ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h
priority_rationale: Finite-type iterate-fixed-point existence from inflationary starts is a technical support theorem.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y})
summary_delta: The verified counterexample shows the failed theorem breaks specifically because Henkin uses preorder equivalence rather than equality. Adding antisymmetry tests whether that is the exact missing assumption, and if true it would convert the refuted statement into a sharp boundary theorem rather than another local repair.
priority_rationale: This antisymmetry repair is a narrow technical salvage of a refuted finite-orbit criterion.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ↔ x = y) → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : α, g = ⊠ g)
summary_delta: The counterexample works because in the equality-order regime, Gödel existence collapses exactly to ordinary fixed-point existence for `⊠`. Isolating that criterion would explain the obstruction exposed by the refutation in a reusable way and provide a clean interface theorem for future counterexample building, rather than merely recording another isolated model.
priority_rationale: This equality-order interface is tidy but too specialized to change the current summary.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)
summary_delta: This is the natural preorder-stable replacement suggested by the counterexample: equality-orbit finiteness was too strong, but repetition up to `≐` matches the actual notion of Henkin fixedness. It also directly advances the active frontier calling for an exact ungated per-`k` orbit-repetition characterization.
priority_rationale: This is a strong nongated per-`k` orbit-repeat characterization and directly addresses a named frontier gap.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ((⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
summary_delta: The counterexample makes clear that under `C5` the surviving existence boundary is tied to the behavior of `⊠` at top, not to parity. An exact top-based criterion would isolate the obstruction exposed by the refutation in a reusable interface statement and provide the right bridge for future finite and infinite classification work.
priority_rationale: A top-based criterion under `C5` could sharpen the current Gödel existence interface, though it is less urgent than the subtype-equivalence target.

stmt: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ ¬ (⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ∧ IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n
summary_delta: The verified three-point model suggests a whole counterexample family, not a one-off failure. Proving arbitrary finite-size separation would turn the refutation into a reusable counterexample-construction theorem and sharply show that below `C5`, top-box nonrefutability is far too weak to force Gödel existence.
priority_rationale: An all-size finite counterexample family is interesting, but finite-family expansion is not where the summary most needs movement.

stmt: For any `α` with `ACR α` and `∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)`, in the canonical realization with `□x = x` and `⊠x = ⊤`, every element of `α` is a Henkin fixed point.
summary_delta: This gives the opposite fixed-point extreme from the Gödel side: the canonical model has maximal Henkin abundance while Gödel points are controlled by proximity to `⊤`. That directly interfaces with the active Henkin agenda and provides a clean benchmark family for iterate-based Henkin criteria without leaving the current theory.
priority_rationale: This canonical Henkin abundance statement is now mostly a benchmark corollary.

stmt: For any `α` with `ACR α` and `∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)`, in the canonical realization with `□x = x` and `⊠x = ⊤`, an element `g : α` is a Gödel fixed point exactly when `g ≐ (⊤ : α)`.
summary_delta: The solved theorem only gives existence of one Gödel fixed point. This sharper classification identifies the entire Gödel-fixed locus of the canonical construction, turning a local witness into reusable structure and clarifying how much fixed-point behavior is forced by the underlying order alone.
priority_rationale: This gives the exact Gödel locus in the canonical benchmark family and would improve the theory’s positive interface story.

stmt: ∀ n : ℕ, (∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n) ↔ 2 ≤ n
summary_delta: This is the sharp repaired boundary suggested by the new all-size no-Gödel family: size 1 should be excluded by consistency, so the natural positive-family target is an exact threshold at 2 rather than the current weaker and likely mislocated `1 ≤ n` formulation. Resolving it would replace an unstable existence claim by a clean minimal-cardinality classification in the finite trivial-`□` regime.
priority_rationale: Finite-size threshold classification remains too close to overexplored cardinality bookkeeping.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [Subsingleton α] [ACR α], ¬ ACR.Consistent α
summary_delta: The proof mined the real obstruction as carrier collapse, not finiteness. Isolating the subsingleton boundary removes the inessential `Fintype` hypothesis and yields a reusable structural criterion for inconsistency that can feed later threshold and separation arguments.
priority_rationale: This is already essentially absorbed by the verified nontriviality and subsingleton inconsistency classifications.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α], letI := Classical.choose (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α)); ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α)
summary_delta: The solved theorem now gives the benchmark canonical trivial-`□` model on every nontrivial bounded linear order. The next frontier is not mere existence of one Gödel point but an exact description of the whole Gödel locus; in this canonical regime, showing that every Gödel fixed point is exactly top would turn the new construction into a reusable classification result and directly advances the missing bridge from realization existence to fixed-point structure.
priority_rationale: This statement is already verified as a derived theorem.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α], letI := Classical.choose (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α)); ∀ x : α, ∃ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 = x
summary_delta: This isolates the Henkin side of the canonical trivial-`□` benchmark family that the proof made available on all bounded linear orders. A theorem that every element is realized by a Henkin fixed point would give the exact Henkin locus rather than another existence lemma, matching the agenda pressure for full fixed-point classifications in the benchmark models and providing a clean base case for later iterate-dynamics results.
priority_rationale: This would complete the Henkin-locus description in the canonical benchmark family and is a useful positive interface theorem.

stmt: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α ≃ α)
summary_delta: The proof logs show that universal `□`-fixedness does more than give nonemptiness and pointwise realization: it identifies the entire Henkin fixed-point subtype with the carrier. This is a clean structural interface theorem for the active fixed-point locus frontier, and it compresses the solved result into a reusable equivalence rather than another local existence reformulation.
priority_rationale: Turning universal `□`-fixedness into a subtype equivalence for Henkin points is a clean interface upgrade over the current pointwise realization theorem.

stmt: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x ∧ x ≐ ⊠x) ↔ Nonempty α
summary_delta: The verified construction already builds a stronger extreme model than the solved statement used: on every nonempty carrier one can realize both universal Henkin and universal Gödel fixedness at once. Making that explicit would add a clean benchmark on the positive side and sharpen the theory’s picture of how far trivial fixed-point collapse can be pushed before consistency is imposed.
priority_rationale: This extreme benchmark package is structurally neat but less urgent than the current Gödel and iterate interface gaps.
